Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mild hypercreatininemia, proteinuria, glycosuria, ketonuria, and low urine specific gravity, with cloudy, brown, ammoniacal urine but no leukocytes or nitrites. Blood glucose and HbA1c are low-normal, suggesting no chronic hyperglycemia. Findings may indicate possible renal dysfunction (tubular/glomerular), and the urine abnormalities could suggest acute kidney injury or nephrotic syndrome. Recommend further renal evaluation and correlation with clinical findings.
